rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/15/2024, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The proposed order must be complete in itself without reference to the petition or exhibits. LRSF 14.7(A)(2) & (3). A specific amount of cash proposed to be paid or distributed, together with the name and share of each distributee and assignee, must be set forth in the body of the proposed order. 2) Exhibit C must list a specific amount of reduction and assignment to each assignee. 3) Re Exhibit E: i) The original payee must be disclosed as to each reimbursement. ii) The petitioner must file a creditor claim (DE-172 form) to seek the Court's approval for reimbursement of funeral expenses. See sections 9000(a)(3) & 9252; see also LRSF 14.29. iii) The attorney's filing fees and copying fees paid to the Court must be itemized. 4) Please provide a copy of notice of proposed action given before donating the estate's tangible persona property. See section 10513. The information required by CRC 7.250(a) must also be provided. 5) The estate cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. Please provide a proof that the cash is now fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). 6) Is the estate ready to be closed, even though the administrator has not recovered proceeds of Macy shares? Note: An appearance may be required on 07/15/2024, if papers showing satisfactory progress to address those issues are not filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID PACHECO JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2024. The following preliminary issues to be cleared two weeks prior: 1. Petition to suggest the proposed distribution to the minors to be distributed to the court-appointed guardian of the estate of each child. Proposed distribution suggests UGMA; however, guardianships have been established. 2. Petition to set forth the age and the date of birth of each minor beneficiary. 3. Need allegation whether the GAL has been paid or waived. 4. Need a verification of the ending balance of assets. LRSF 14.34.D. 5. Losses (-$17.83) have not been reflected in the fee base. The amount may not affect the stat compensation amount; however, paragraph #30 statement saying "no loss" conflicts the summary of account. 6. Need allegation as to RSN. Even if the RSN filing party has been notified, paragraph #10 makes an inaccurate allegation. 7. Need details for attorney's cost advanced. $1,690.50 being disbursed to the counsel. 8. Need further information re disbursement of $1,000 to Catherine Boles. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARINA NIKOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was not given to Marta Eisenberg, the Court-appointed attorney for Conservatee. (2) As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] (3) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) For the first account, the conservator must also file all original account statements showing the account balance immediately preceding the date the conservator was appointed. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] (5) Paragraphs 5-7 of the petition do not disclose that a "Partial No. 2 Amended" I&A was filed on 11/17/23, which nevertheless appears to have been a consolidated Corrected Partial No. 1 and No. 2. No proof of mailing of notice of how to object to that I&A appears to have been filed. (6) Paragraph 7 of the petition states that the aggregate value of the estate is $1,486,953.22, but paragraph 9 shows the starting total as $1,470,549.33. (7) That summary of account in paragraph 9 of the petition does not balance. (8) Paragraph 10 was blank, and the "supplemental declaration" filed on 4/26/24 AMENDED the petition. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Is the conservator seeking reimbursement for those funds? This is not in the prayer. The dates of services (months) were not in the description of those payments listed on the liabilities schedule. Copies of the invoices from the facility might need to be filed to confirm the amounts paid. (9) Where on the informational schedule is the acquisition of the GE Healthcare shares shown? (10) What were the "pre-authorized auto-transfer to savings" amounts to the conservator? Authorized by whom? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: The procedures for how to make a remote appearance should be attached to the Notice of Hearing. Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN GO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/24. The following issues to be cleared before the hearing: (1) No authority under which the court can grant the transfer of the asset to the surviving spouse directly when the decedent's will expressly leaves the asset to the trust. Probate Code sec. 13501. Petitioner's supplement filed 5/17/24 relies on judicial discretion. --- The court will determine on this issue at the hearing. (2) Need clarification of the disclaimers executed by William and Thomas, respectively. (a) If they have any interest in the real property to disclaim at this time. The asset is allegedly held in the decedent's name as an individual. The trustee is the sole devisee. (b) Setting aside the validity of the disclaimer, petition is silent as to whether William and/or Thomas has any children or not as the disclaimer will treat the disclaimant as if predeceased the decedent, transferring the interest disclaimed to the disclaimant's issues/descendants if any. (3) A copy of the current deed showing how the title is held. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES W. MOSSER 2007 TRUST DATED JUNE 10, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice with a copy of the petition must be given to all beneficiaries of the trust, but anyone whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition must be personally served; e.g., heirs who are not trust beneficiaries. [Probate Code Section 17203(b).] (4) Notice must be given directly to an interested party, not only to his attorney, unless the party entitled to notice has directed in writing that notice may be given only to his attorney. [CRC 7.51(a).] (5) A courtesy copy of the petition and related papers also were not provided, so the petition has not been reviewed in full detail; however, the trust appears to name other beneficiaries, and the petition is silent about their interests. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL TAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 5/30/24 at 12:50 p.m.* Continued to 6/17/2024. The following issue is cleared a week before. (1) Need a verified supplement stating further information regarding the adoption such as the date of the adoption, case number, and so on. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD PINEIRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petitioner might wish to petition for Letters of Special Administration for the purpose of obtaining a death certificate and/or hiring an heir search company. Without further, specific information about the decedent's heirs, predeceased or otherwise, he will not be appointed. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
